body_txt: When UFOs fly over a farm, it always seems that the first thing they do is to beam up the farmer's cows with their Tractor Beam . Abduction accounts being what they are, you'd think aliens would be more interested in scientists or political leaders than farmers, but where else would you test a tractor beam? This is actually a great example of a Dead Unicorn Trope . Like Anal Probing , "Cattle Abductions" were never really suspected alien encounters in the first place; but they've become popular as a sort of satirical combination of cattle-mutilation stories (compare the similar urban legend of el chupacabras ) and Alien Abduction stories. It's also a useful trope for designating aliens that are up to no good, but in a comedic or nonthreatening way (the sight gag of a mooing cow being tractor-beamed up into a flying saucer). Sometimes it's even played as a kind of obsessive-compulsive disorder: they just can't resist beaming the creature up. Sub-Trope of Sky Heist . Not to Be Confused with Aliens Steal Cable — although if they've been watching too many Westerns , that may be where they got the idea .
